pi_items.source: Verified against task.json description (line 5: 'save the file as library_er_diagram.drawio'), setup_task.sh (line 22, 25), and export_result.sh (line 9). Confirmed as valid draw.io file extension format.

Verified against task.json description (line 5: 'on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop)'), setup_task.sh (lines 22, 25-26), and export_result.sh (line 9). Confirmed as valid Linux user desktop directory path.

Verified against task.json description (lines 1-4: 'BOOK entity', 'AUTHOR entity', 'MEMBER entity', 'LOAN entity'). Metadata contains lowercase normalized versions of the entity names as specified in the description.
Verified from task.json description critical requirement (line 1: 'All 4 entities must be present'). Each entity represents one shape in the ER diagram.

Verified from task.json description (line 3: 'At least 3 connections between entities') with specific connections listed: BOOK-AUTHOR, BOOK-LOAN, MEMBER-LOAN. Confirmed in verifier.py line 91 which checks num_connections >= min_connections.

Verified from task.json description critical requirement (line 2: 'Each entity must have at least 2 visible attributes/fields'). Confirmed in export_result.sh entity detection logic and task description.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating an Entity-Relationship diagram in draw.io with 4 entities (Book, Author, Member, Loan), each with at least 2 attributes, and at least 3 connections between entities (Book-Author, Book-Loan, Member-Loan). The file must be saved as 'library_er_diagram.drawio' on the Desktop at /home/ga/Desktop. The .drawio format is XML-based. All entity names and structural requirements are consistent across the task description, setup scripts, and verification logic. Data is synthetically generated by the user during task execution.
